children.name: Cryptobranchus alleganiensis
children.description: Largest North American amphibian at up to 74 cm; folds of loose lateral skin absorb oxygen from fast-flowing, well-oxygenated streams; male guards the nest for 3 months; declining severely due to sedimentation and chytrid disease.

children.name: Andrias japonicus
children.description: Up to 1.5 m long; fully aquatic; Japanese Special Natural Monument; guards an underwater nest burrow; nocturnal; lives over 50 years.

children.name: Andrias davidianus
children.description: World's largest amphibian at up to 1.8 m and 64 kg; critically endangered in the wild; heavily farmed for food in China; some documented individuals more than a century old.
